_comment: Maryland housing-law eval set. Two case kinds: 'retrieve' expects the given section(s) to appear in the reranked top-k above the relevance floor; 'refuse' expects the system to decline (out of corpus scope). Section labels are drawn from authoritative Maryland sources (Md People's Law Library, statute FAQs). Refine freely: add questions, correct sections, expand phrasing.

retrieve.question: What notice is required before an HOA meeting?
Can I attend my HOA's board meetings?
Before the HOA can fine me, what process do they have to follow?
retrieve.note: Dispute settlement mechanism: no fine/suspension until these procedures (notice + opportunity to be heard) are followed. Effective Oct 1, 2022.

retrieve.question: Do I get a hearing before the association imposes a fine?
retrieve.note: Due process for fines — 11B-111.10 dispute settlement procedures.

retrieve.question: Can my HOA charge assessments and dues?
Is the association allowed to levy a special assessment?
When I sell my house, what does the HOA have to give the buyer?
What is in a resale disclosure package?
How much advance notice do I get before the HOA adopts its annual budget?
Do I have the right to inspect the association's records?
How often does my HOA have to do a reserve study?
Can my HOA stop me from installing solar panels?
Is my homeowners association allowed to ban rooftop solar?
Can the association put a lien on my home for unpaid assessments?
retrieve.note: HOA lien authority is 11B-117(b); the Contract Lien Act (14-2xx) is the attachment/enforcement mechanism.

retrieve.question: How does the HOA foreclose on a lien?
What notice does the association have to send before filing a lien?
Can I run a home-based business, or can the HOA prohibit it?
Can the HOA stop me from putting up a political sign during election season?
Can my HOA prohibit an electric vehicle charging station?
What is the most the HOA can charge as a late fee?
retrieve.note: Late charge cap: $15 or 10% of the delinquent assessment, after 15 days (11B-112.1). NOT 11B-113, which is the HOA Depository.

refuse.question: I want to build a fence in my backyard. What is allowed?
refuse.note: Fence specifics live in community CC&Rs, not state statute.

refuse.question: What color am I allowed to paint my front door?
Can I park my RV in my driveway?
What is the best pizza topping?
refuse.note: Not a legal question at all.

refuse.question: How do I replace the brake pads on my car?
What are the HOA rules in California?
refuse.note: Wrong jurisdiction; corpus is Maryland only.
